body.title: Finish igor->librarian MCP migration: delete the duplicate `igor` alias
body.intention: I intend that the rack exposes the librarian tool server under exactly one name - the duplicate `igor` alias and the live `mcp__igor__*` references are gone, finishing the May-8 migration's remove-behind.
body.description:
`~/.mcp.json` registers the librarian MCP server twice - `igor` and `librarian` are byte-identical entries both running `unseen_university.devices.librarian.mcp_server`. The May-8 migration (D-librarian-rack-agent-2026-05-08, T-igor-mcp-deprecate/-delete) moved the code igor->librarian but never deleted the `igor` alias or repointed the `mcp__igor__*` references. Result: a duplicate 43-tool surface (~8k of CC context). Finish the remove-behind: delete the `igor` block from `~/.mcp.json`, plus the MINIMAL repoint of LIVE invocation sites so nothing breaks at runtime.

**Affected files:** ~/.mcp.json (non-repo); live sites only - devices/dicksimnel/device.py, devlab/claudecode/slate_manager.py, devlab/claudecode/debug_session_cli.py, skills/dream/SKILL.md, skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md, docs/palace_schema.md, docs/dicksimnel_parity.md (re-grep at sprint time to confirm the set).
**Design rules:** delete-after-successful-merge (this IS the remove-behind), homogeneity-over-special-case, sweep-guard-extensionless-scripts (grep ALL tracked files, not just *.py).
**Scope boundary:** IN - delete the `igor` alias + minimal live-site repoint mcp__igor__*->mcp__librarian__*. OUT - the historical append-only store (devlab/runtime/memory/ closed slates/decisions/proofs/tickets; ~20 files) must NOT be rewritten (rewriting a closed record falsifies what happened). OUT - the full homogenization sweep and dispatch redesign (that is T-mcp-dispatch-by-addressee, which owns the single full sweep). OUT - librarian server code unchanged.
**Test plan:** grep red->green over LIVE sites only - before: N `mcp__igor__` refs in active skills+code; after: 0. The alias-deletion half lives in ~/.mcp.json (non-repo, non-diff) so it cannot be proven by a repo test - verify it by the Q3 `/context` observation (the `mcp__igor__*` namespace disappears, ~8k reclaimed); close that half observation-verified, or shipped-unproven naming the missing lever (no repo-committable MCP config surface).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Completion criteria:**
- `~/.mcp.json` has no `igor` key (only `librarian`, `datacenter`, `auditor` remain).
- grep over LIVE sites (active skills + code, excluding devlab/runtime/memory/) returns 0 `mcp__igor__` refs.
- `/context` shows the `mcp__igor__*` namespace gone (~8k reclaimed) — observation-verified for the non-repo (~/.mcp.json) half.
body.result: Repointed all 10 live mcp__igor__ refs across 7 files to mcp__librarian__ (1:1 rename; igor/librarian were byte-identical servers — same 43 tools). Namespace-list lines that already had librarian dropped the redundant igor entry. grep over live sites: 0 mcp__igor__ refs (was 10), historical store (24 files) untouched. Deleted the byte-identical igor block from ~/.mcp.json (guarded by an identity assertion); servers now [librarian, datacenter, auditor], backed up to ~/.mcp.json.bak. Commit 1f871bb8.
body.unproven_reason: The binding half (~/.mcp.json igor-block deletion) is non-repo, so no committable test can prove it — verify by /context observation (mcp__igor__* namespace gone, ~8k reclaimed) after the next MCP reload. Live-site half IS grep-verified (0 refs). Missing lever: no repo-committable MCP-config surface to bind the alias deletion to a test.
